steps.title: Identify what the underlined portion actually says
steps.content: The underlined phrase is "—more than 7,000 in all—" — a parenthetical dash phrase inserted after "CEP attendees." It provides a concrete participant count. The passage's opening claim is that the CEP was "crucial" to the Civil Rights Movement's success. A participant number logically underscores scale of impact, not opinions, topics, or criticism.

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — It suggests that CEP attendees held a diverse array of opinions about the Southern Christian Leadership Conference's political philosophy.: A headcount tells you nothing about attendees' opinions. ✗
  • Choice B — It illustrates the CEP organizers' efforts to educate participants on a wide variety of topics.: The variety of topics is described elsewhere in the sentence; a number alone does not illustrate topic breadth. ✗
  • Choice C — It establishes that criticism of the CEP was limited to a few individuals in the Southern Christian Leadership Conference.: No criticism of the CEP is mentioned in the passage. ✗
  • Choice D — It underscores the extent of the CEP's impact on the Civil Rights Movement of the 1960s.: Over 7,000 participants trained reinforces the passage's claim that the CEP was "crucial" — a large number directly supports the extent of impact. ✓
That is the correct answer.
